LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS XTRONX, INC., XTRONX T2, L.L.C. AND ERIC WONG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE STANDING AS REQUIRED BY BOHN V SMITH (1967) 252 CAL APP 2D 678, 682. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT, WONG'S AGENCY, AND THE CONTRACT CLAIM IS NOT UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF'S ALTER EGO CLAIM REQUIRES ADDITIONAL FACTS TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE CONTENTION OF UNITY OF OWNERSHIP. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Plaintiff Peggy Snyder'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Peggy Snyder's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents (defendant Xtronx Inc.) and production of documents is granted. The objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to provide supplemetnal responses and produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. If any documents are withheld pursuant to either the attorney client or attorney work product privilege, they shall be identified in a privilege log. The motion as to defendant (Xtronx T2 LLC is moot. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
